# Developer Workflows

Document the commands and workflows developers and agents should use.

The main developer workflow is intentionally command-line first.

Use `wk init` to create the store and generated wiki pages. Use `wk status --json` and `wk spin --json` at the start of agent work. Use add commands for new records, or `wk record update` and `wk record delete` for append-only revisions.

Use `wk render` when structured records should refresh the deterministic wiki pages. Use `wk search <query> --json` when an agent needs local context.

For human-facing docs, use the compile workflow:

1. `wk compile draft --role all --json`
2. Let the active IDE agent revise the draft Markdown in `.wikiwiki/drafts/compile/<draft-id>/`.
3. `wk compile apply <draft-id> --json`
4. Run `wk validate`.

That split is important. Drafts are where agent-authored prose happens; apply is where provenance is checked and published.
